hypothesis: The `LR=4e-4` exploitation test clearly underperformed on the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` `K=32, EF=12` anchor, so the settled learning rate should remain `3.2e-3`. The next clean one-variable exploitation step is to sweep `AUXK_ALPHA` on that same anchor, and the most informative remaining value is `0.01`, which tests whether a smaller nonzero auxiliary pressure helps without the regression seen at `0.03125`.
summary: Run a pure aux sweep on the current best `lowrank_two_stage_residual` anchor at `K=32, EF=12` by changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.0` to `0.01` while keeping architecture, optimizer, learning rate, and all other settings fixed. This follows the exploitation guidance after the failed LR sweep.
self_review: This is the most policy-compliant follow-up under continued stagnation: stay on the best active family, keep the frontier anchor fixed, and change exactly one scalar parameter. `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` is a sensible next probe because it is the mildest untested nonzero aux value and can reveal whether the previous `0.03125` miss was due to overshooting rather than aux being uniformly harmful.
notes_to_memory: Round 39 continues exploitation on the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` `K=32, EF=12` anchor after `LR=4e-4` clearly underperformed, keeping `LR=3.2e-3` as the settled value.

The next one-variable test is `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01`, chosen as the mildest remaining nonzero aux sweep to determine whether the prior `0.03125` regression was due to excessive auxiliary pressure rather than aux being categorically harmful.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` improves the `K=32` anchor, propagate that aux setting to one adjacent frontier K before any further scalar sweeps.
If it clearly underperforms, keep `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` as the best settled value and test the remaining `0.0625` only if policy still requires exhausting the standard sweep set.
If it is close but noisy, prioritize one confirmatory rerun of the better aux setting before changing another parameter.
